---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
# The Adelaide School
## Learning Sciences, Data & AI

An intensive two-day forum for researchers, educators, and practitioners working at the intersection of learning, data, and artificial intelligence.

::: {.badge-date}
Coming 2026 · Adelaide University
:::
:::

## What is The Adelaide School?

The Adelaide School is a two-day intensive event hosted by the [Centre for Change and Complexity in Learning (C3L)](https://c3l.edu.au) at Adelaide University. It brings together researchers, educators, and practitioners to share methods, exchange ideas, and build the collaborations that move the field forward.

This is not a conference. There are no passive audiences, no keynotes delivered from a distance. The Adelaide School is designed around participation — structured sessions where you bring your work, your questions, and your half-formed ideas, and leave with something concrete.

## Who is it for?

- Early and mid-career researchers in education, learning sciences, psychology, and adjacent fields
- Practitioners working with data in school, university, or workplace learning contexts
- Research students looking to build skills in methods, AI tools, and grant strategy
- Anyone curious about what learning sciences and AI can do together

## What to expect

Two days structured around three things: **learning new methods and tools**, **sharing your own work**, and **making connections** with people you want to work with.

→ [See the full program](program.qmd)

→ [Browse the sessions](sessions.qmd)

## Hosted by

The Adelaide School is an initiative of **C3L — the Centre for Change and Complexity in Learning** at Adelaide University, with sessions led by C3L researchers and invited collaborators from across Australia and beyond.
